title: Optimizely Opal Agent Spec
description: Schema for Nansen's portable Opal agent definitions. Validates agent specs for use across clients, GitHub storage, and Opal import.

schema_version.description: Schema version for this spec format.

name.description: Human-readable display name shown in Opal UI. Convention: [Team - Solution] Agent Name.

version.description: Semantic version of this agent definition.

description.description: 2-5 sentence description of the agent's purpose and functionality.

prompt_template.description: Full CLEAR-structured prompt template with [[variable]] placeholders.

type.description: Opal parameter type. IMPORTANT: Opal only accepts these four types on import. For array/list data, use 'object' (Opal treats complex JSON inputs as objects) or 'string' (pass as serialized JSON string).

default.description: Default value. Null means no default.

required.description: Whether the parameter must be provided at runtime.

description.description: What this parameter represents and how it's used.

schema.description: JSON shape the agent must produce. Keys are field names, values describe types.

description.description: What the output is used for downstream.

inference_type.description: Opal inference level setting. 'simple' = 1 tool call max. 'standard' = multi-step. 'advanced' = most capable.

creativity.description: Opal creativity setting (0.0 = deterministic, 1.0 = maximum creativity).

file_urls.description: Reference files attached to this agent.

is_active.description: Whether this agent definition is active (not archived).

is_enabled_in_chat.description: Whether the agent appears in Opal chat for end users. Set false for workflow-only specialized agents.

internal_version.description: Opal's internal revision counter (auto-incremented on save in Opal UI). Never hand-edit.

agent_metadata.description: For workflows: contains edges and nodes for the visual editor. For specialized: null. Conditional steps generate additional condition nodes here.

samples.description: Example executions for testing and validation.

_nansen.description: Nansen-specific metadata not sent to Opal. Stripped by export script.

client.description: Client slug this instance belongs to (matches folder name).

template_source.description: Template agent_id this was derived from, if any.

tags.description: Freeform tags for grouping and search.

last_synced_to_opal.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last push to Opal instance.

notes.description: Version history in reverse chronological order. E.g., 'v0.2.0: Fixed X.\nv0.1.0: Initial.'

rc_date.description: Date the agent entered RC status (YYYY-MM-DD format).

_workflow_step_types.description: Reference for workflow step types used in the steps array. Not part of the agent schema itself.

conditional.notes: parameters_schema should be null (not {}). No next_step_id — routing is handled by conditions.

condition_fields.matching_condition: String pattern to match against evaluator output
condition_fields.target_step_id: UUID of the step to execute if condition matches
condition_fields.priority: Evaluation order (lower = first). Opal often sets all to 0.
